title: Division operator / without DIVIDE — no BLANK/error handling
description: The / operator returns an error on division by zero. DIVIDE returns BLANK (or an alternate result) on division by zero, which is almost always the desired behavior in reports.

title: SWITCH without default/else value
description: SWITCH without a default value returns BLANK when no condition matches. This is often unintentional and can cause confusing report behavior.
fix_suggestion: Add a default value as the last argument to SWITCH, e.g., SWITCH(expr, val1, res1, val2, res2, defaultResult)

title: Potential BLANK propagation in arithmetic
description: Expressions like 1 - (x/y) or 1 - DIVIDE(...) may return unexpected results when the denominator is BLANK, because arithmetic with BLANK propagates as 0.

match.description: Arithmetic subtraction from literal involving DIVIDE or division

fix_suggestion: Use VAR to capture intermediate results and check for BLANK explicitly with IF(ISBLANK(...))

title: IFERROR forces step-by-step evaluation
description: IFERROR must evaluate the expression first and then check for errors, preventing the engine from optimizing the execution plan. Use DIVIDE for division errors, or handle edge cases explicitly.
fix_suggestion: Replace with DIVIDE for division errors, or use IF with explicit condition checks

title: ISERROR forces step-by-step evaluation
description: ISERROR evaluates the full expression to check for errors, preventing query optimization. Handle specific error conditions explicitly instead.
fix_suggestion: Check for the specific condition that causes the error (e.g., division by zero, missing relationship) instead of catching all errors

title: SUMMARIZE with inline expressions — use ADDCOLUMNS+SUMMARIZE or SUMMARIZECOLUMNS
description: SUMMARIZE with more than 2 arguments likely contains inline aggregation expressions. This deprecated pattern can produce incorrect results when the aggregation references columns not in the groupBy set.
fix_suggestion: Use ADDCOLUMNS(SUMMARIZE(table, groupByCol), "Name", expression) or SUMMARIZECOLUMNS

title: INTERSECT — consider TREATAS for virtual relationships
description: INTERSECT materializes both tables. When used for filtering, TREATAS creates a virtual relationship without materialization, which is more efficient and clearer in intent.
fix_suggestion: If used for filtering, replace with TREATAS(VALUES(col), targetTable[col])

title: Hardcoded filter value in CALCULATE
description: Hardcoded filter values in CALCULATE (e.g., Table[Year] = 2024) make measures brittle and require updates when data changes.

match.description: CALCULATE with hardcoded literal comparison in filter

fix_suggestion: Parameterize with slicers, use relative date logic, or use a disconnected table for dynamic filtering
